Name: traffic-ticket-contest
Description: A production-grade ERB platform (admin/state-machine/jurisdiction/routing/feature-catalog machinery, extracted from the industrial-ui-services portal) with traffic-ticket contest as the example domain. The platform tables carry the full production conventions; tickets are one domain riding on top.

_meta._CMCC_Summary: Airtable export with schema-first type mapping: Schemas, Data, Relationships (FK links), Lookups (INDEX/MATCH), Aggregations (SUMIFS/COUNTIFS/Rollups), and Calculated fields (formulas) in Excel dialect. Field types are determined from Airtable's schema metadata FIRST (no coercion), with intelligent fallback to formula/data analysis only when schema is unavailable.

_conversion_metadata.field_type_mapping: checkbox→boolean, number→number/integer, multipleRecordLinks→relationship, multipleLookupValues→lookup, rollup→aggregation, count→aggregation, formula→calculated

type_inference.priority: airtable_metadata (NO COERCION) → formula_analysis → reference_resolution → data_analysis (fallback only)
type_inference.airtable_type_mapping: checkbox→boolean, singleLineText→string, multilineText→string, number→number/integer, datetime→datetime, singleSelect→string, email→string, url→string, phoneNumber→string
type_inference.data_coercion_hierarchy: Only used as fallback when Airtable schema unavailable: datetime → number → integer → boolean → base64 → json → string

schema.name: Depth
schema.Description: Calculated — nesting depth in the menu tree. 0 = top-level (no parent); otherwise the number of dot-segments in the dotted RouteKey above the root (e.g. 'library.rules.detail' = 2). Drives N-deep indentation.

schema.name: FullPath
schema.Description: Calculated — canonical role-agnostic URL path (equals Route). The SPA renders this under each permitted role as /:role + FullPath and resolves it only when that role's *CanRead is true; the role is an implicit prefix, never stored on the row.

schema.name: HandlerBaseName
schema.Description: Calculated — space-delimited form of the dotted RouteKey (dots and hyphens become spaces), e.g. 'library rules detail'. The client PascalCases this and prefixes the viewer role to derive the handler component deterministically: {Role} + PascalCase(HandlerBaseName) -> e.g. AdminLibraryRulesDetail, RepresentativeLibraryRulesDetail. No per-role handler is stored; edge cases get their own single-role route row instead.

schema.Description: Display name of the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: BaseFineUsd
schema.Description: Base fine for the cited violation, pulled from the ViolationType.

schema.name: ViolationPoints
schema.Description: License points for the cited violation, pulled from the ViolationType.

schema.name: IssuedOn
schema.Description: Date the citation was issued to the driver.

schema.name: AsOfDate
schema.Description: The 'today' reference date used to evaluate deadlines for this citation (lets seed data exercise overdue/late states deterministically).

schema.name: RespondedOn
schema.Description: Date the driver responded (paid or requested a hearing). Null if no response yet.

schema.name: ContestRequested
schema.Description: Whether the driver elected to contest the citation (request a hearing) rather than pay.

schema.name: PaidOn
schema.Description: Date the fine was paid in full. Null if not yet paid.

schema.name: AmountPaidUsd
schema.Description: Total amount the driver has paid against this citation. Null/0 if nothing paid.

schema.name: DaysToRespond
schema.Description: Response window in days, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: DaysToPayAfterRuling
schema.Description: Days-to-pay window, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: LatePenaltyPct
schema.Description: Late-penalty percentage, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: DaysLateToCollections
schema.Description: Days-late-to-collections window, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: ResponseDueDate
schema.Description: Deadline to respond: IssuedOn + the jurisdiction's response window.

schema.name: DaysUntilResponseDue
schema.Description: Days remaining (negative if overdue) until the response deadline, measured from AsOfDate.

schema.name: IsResponseOverdue
schema.Description: True when no response was filed and the response deadline has passed as of AsOfDate.

schema.name: CountOfHearings
schema.Description: Number of hearing records attached to this citation.

schema.name: LatestHearingOutcome
schema.Description: Outcome of the most recent hearing for this citation (Pending if a hearing exists but has no outcome; blank if no hearing).

schema.name: ContestStatus
schema.Description: Contest/Hearing state machine: NotContested when the driver did not elect to contest; otherwise HearingRequested -> Scheduled -> Heard, reflected from the latest hearing's outcome.

schema.name: IsDismissed
schema.Description: True when the latest hearing outcome dismissed the citation.

schema.name: IsGuilty
schema.Description: True when the driver is liable: either found guilty/upheld at hearing, or defaulted by missing the response deadline without contesting.

schema.name: AmountDueUsd
schema.Description: Amount currently owed: 0 if dismissed; otherwise the base fine plus the jurisdiction's late penalty if the payment is late.

schema.name: PaymentDueDate
schema.Description: Date the fine is due: the later anchor (response deadline) plus the jurisdiction's days-to-pay window.

schema.name: IsPaymentLate
schema.Description: True when the driver is liable, has not paid in full, and the payment due date has passed as of AsOfDate.

schema.name: IsInCollections
schema.Description: True when a late payment has remained unpaid past the jurisdiction's collections window.

schema.name: PaymentStatus
schema.Description: Payment/Penalty state machine: NotOwed (dismissed) -> Paid -> Collections -> Late -> Due. Evaluated in priority order.

schema.name: EffectivePoints
schema.Description: License points this citation actually contributes to the driver: the violation's points if the driver is liable and not dismissed, otherwise 0. Drives the driver's ActivePoints rollup.

schema.name: CitationStatus
schema.Description: Citation lifecycle state machine: Issued -> Responded -> InContest -> Adjudicated -> Closed. The top-level status synthesizing the other tracks.

schema.name: Hearings
schema.Description: Reverse FK: hearing records for this citation.

data.StringValue: A traffic ticket everyone understands, modeled as four state machines and a multi-jurisdiction rules engine.

A driver gets a citation. They can pay it or contest it at a hearing. Miss the deadline and you default; lose the hearing and you owe; pay late and a jurisdiction-specific penalty applies; rack up points and your license is warned then suspended. Every deadline, fine, penalty, point cap, and threshold is a row in the Jurisdictions table — change a jurisdiction's rule and every citation under it re-derives. The four state machines (Citation, Contest, Payment, License) are calculated fields, never stored-and-mutated.

data.JsonValue: ["Show how a familiar, deadline-driven case flow decomposes into calculated state machines","Demonstrate a multi-jurisdiction rules engine where regulations are data, not code","Illustrate an append-only event log coexisting with computed current state"]

["Citation: Issued -> Responded -> InContest -> Adjudicated -> Closed","Contest: NotContested | HearingRequested -> Scheduled -> Heard","Payment: Pending -> Due -> Late -> Collections | Paid | NotOwed","License: Valid -> Warning -> Suspended"]
